title: The Root Chorus

features.name: the eldest tap-root
features.desc-on-touch: it goes down further than memory; older than the archive, it sings lower than the range of your hearing but the ground remembers the vibration

features.name: convergent tangle
features.desc-on-touch: where roots from four old trees have grown into and around each other; in the tangled wood, their voices become one chorus that no single tree could make

features.name: nutrient mirror
features.desc-on-touch: the soil here reflects what the roots draw: you see what nourishment looks like when it has just begun to sing

features.name: the ancient crown
features.desc-on-touch: deep underground, a root-crown so vast it stretches beyond what any single tree claims; it thinks in the plural

features.name: echo-cavity
features.desc-on-touch: a hollow chamber where the oldest root sings and the smaller roots answer; here you hear the geometry of agreement

inscription: beneath the archive, older voices sing the song that trees learned long before they had rings to count the years

file.name: Root_Song_Spectrum_Analysis_Infrasonic.wav
file.note: field recording from below; the frequency too low for human ear, but the ground shakes with it

exits.label: back east to the stone chamber where the singing is heard above ground
deeper south to the bedrock where even root and stone converge
west into the younger growth, where the new trees still learn the chorus

reverie.title: REVERIE — beneath the eucalyptus, listening
narr: you sit between the roots, the bark warm against your spine,
and Katita beside you hums so low you feel it more than hear it.
the ground beneath hums back, older frequencies she cannot reach.
she grins: the trees were singing before we knew what singing was.
here, under the crown, all of them make one sound together.
